title: Email notifications arriving hours late
body: Task assignment notifications used to arrive within a minute or two. For the past week, they've been arriving 2-4 hours late. This is causing people to miss deadlines because they don't know they've been assigned a task until hours later. It seems to be worse in the morning — notifications sent around 9-10am take the longest to arrive. Afternoon notifications are usually faster but still delayed.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed report — the timing pattern you noticed (worse in the morning, better in the afternoon) is a really helpful clue.

To help us investigate, could you share some environment details? Specifically:
- Which version of TaskFlow are you running (or is this on the cloud-hosted version)?
- What email provider are you using (e.g., Gmail, Outlook, self-hosted)?
- Has anything changed recently in your setup — an app update, a change in email configuration, or a new mail server?

This will help us narrow down whether the delay is on the TaskFlow side or the email delivery side.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: We're on TaskFlow v2.3.1. Email goes through our company's SMTP relay — I'm not sure of the exact details on that, our IT team set it up. As for changes, I think there was some kind of product update about a week ago? I started getting these daily digest emails around the same time the delays started, but I'm not sure if that's related or just a coincidence. Nothing changed on our email server side as far as I know.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for that info — the timing around the product update is very useful, and the daily digest emails you mentioned are an interesting detail.

Could you walk me through the exact steps to reproduce the delay? For example:
1. You assign a task to someone (or get assigned a task) at a specific time.
2. You check your email and note when the notification arrives.
3. The gap is consistently 2-4 hours.

Also, about those daily digest emails — are the delayed notifications arriving as **individual emails** (one per task assignment), or are they being **bundled into the digest**? It's possible that the update enabled a digest feature that's batching your notifications instead of sending them immediately. If you check your TaskFlow notification settings (usually under Settings → Notifications), do you see any option for "email digest" or "batch notifications" that might have been turned on?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: The delayed notifications are individual emails, not bundled into the digest. They're separate — I get the digest email in the morning (which is a summary of activity), and then the task assignment notifications come as their own individual emails, they're just arriving really late. I checked my notification settings and I don't see anything about batching — it's set to send immediately. So the assignments are being sent as individual emails, they're just taking forever to actually show up in my inbox, especially if someone assigns me something in the morning.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Email notifications delayed 2-4 hours after v2.3.1 update, worst during morning hours","problem":"Task assignment email notifications that previously arrived within 1-2 minutes are now delayed by 2-4 hours. The delays began approximately one week ago, coinciding with a product update to v2.3.1 that also introduced daily digest emails. Delays are worst for notifications sent around 9-10am and improve in the afternoon. Notifications arrive as individual emails (not bundled into the digest), and the user's notification settings are configured for immediate delivery.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The v2.3.1 update likely introduced a daily digest/batch email processing job that is interfering with the immediate notification queue. The morning spike suggests the digest job runs in the morning and either monopolizes the email sending worker, clogs the SMTP connection pool, or introduces a queue backlog that individual notifications get stuck behind. As the digest backlog clears through the day, afternoon notifications process faster.","reproduction_steps":["Use TaskFlow v2.3.1 with email notifications set to 'send immediately'","Assign a task to a user in the morning (around 9-10am)","Note the timestamp of the assignment in TaskFlow","Check the recipient's inbox and note when the notification email arrives","Observe a 2-4 hour delay between assignment and email delivery","Repeat in the afternoon and observe a shorter (but still present) delay"],"environment":"TaskFlow v2.3.1 (cloud or self-hosted not confirmed), email delivered via company SMTP relay, notification settings set to immediate delivery, daily digest feature active (likely enabled by the update)","severity":"high","impact":"Users are missing task assignment deadlines because they don't learn about assignments for hours. Affects all users relying on email notifications for task assignments, with morning assignments being most severely delayed.","recommended_fix":"Investigate the email sending pipeline introduced or modified in v2.3.1, specifically: (1) Check if the daily digest job shares a queue or worker pool with immediate notifications and is starving them. (2) Check SMTP connection handling — the digest may be holding connections or hitting rate limits that delay individual sends. (3) Consider separating the digest email queue from the immediate notification queue so they don't compete for resources. (4) Check email worker logs for queue depth and processing times around 9-10am vs afternoon.","proposed_test_case":"Send a task assignment notification at 9am (peak digest processing time) and verify it is delivered within 2 minutes. Confirm that the digest job running concurrently does not delay individual notification delivery. Measure queue depth and delivery latency for individual notifications before and after separating the queues.","information_gaps":["Exact SMTP relay configuration and any rate limits imposed by the company mail server","Whether other users on the same TaskFlow instance experience the same delays","Server-side email queue logs showing when notifications are enqueued vs. when they are handed off to SMTP","Whether the digest feature was auto-enabled by the v2.3.1 update or manually enabled"]}
